rag_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure that all identifiers used in assumptions and guarantees are visible in the current AADL scope and properly declared or referenced.
Place component type contracts in component type scopes and implementation contracts in implementation scopes, respecting the visibility and binding rules of AADL features.
generic_rules: Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE contracts to avoid type resolution errors.
Ensure that all AGREE expressions match the expected scalar types (int, real, bool) to prevent resolution failures.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers unless explicitly allowed by the AADL model's configured dependencies.
Assignments in AGREE should only reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees should be placed in appropriate component type or implementation scopes based on their scope of validity.
anti_patterns: Do not reference identifiers or types that are not visible in the current AADL scope, as this leads to validation errors.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope unless they are tied to an existing output feature or auxiliary variable.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
